examples.title: TCP Three-Way Handshake Pseudocode
examples.description: Demonstrates the connection establishment process in TCP
examples.code:
// TCP Client Side
FUNCTION connect_to_server(server_ip, port):
 CREATE socket
 GENERATE random initial_sequence_number(seq_num)
 
 // Step 1: Send SYN
 SEND TCP_PACKET(flags=SYN, seq=seq_num)
 
 // Step 2: Receive SYN-ACK
 RECEIVE packet
 IF packet.flags == SYN-ACK THEN
 server_seq = packet.seq + 1
 SEND TCP_PACKET(flags=ACK, seq=seq_num+1, ack=server_seq)
 STATE = ESTABLISHED
 END IF

// TCP Server Side
FUNCTION accept_connection():
 LISTEN on socket
 
 // Step 1: Receive SYN
 RECEIVE packet
 IF packet.flags == SYN THEN
 client_seq = packet.seq
 GENERATE server_sequence_number(srv_seq)
 
 // Step 2: Send SYN-ACK
 SEND TCP_PACKET(flags=SYN-ACK, seq=srv_seq, ack=client_seq+1)
 END IF
 
 // Step 3: Receive ACK
 RECEIVE packet
 IF packet.flags == ACK THEN
 STATE = ESTABLISHED
 END IF
examples.explanation: The three-way handshake ensures both client and server agree on initial sequence numbers and establish connection state. SYN carries initial sequence number, SYN-ACK acknowledges client's sequence and carries server's sequence, ACK confirms server's sequence.

examples.title: UDP Send-Receive Pseudocode
examples.description: Demonstrates simple connectionless communication using UDP
examples.code:
// UDP Sender
FUNCTION send_datagram(data, dest_ip, dest_port):
 CREATE socket (SOCK_DGRAM)
 
 // No connection establishment needed
 CREATE packet WITH:
 - Source Port (assigned by OS)
 - Destination Port
 - Length
 - Data
 - Checksum (optional)
 
 SEND packet TO dest_ip:dest_port
 CLOSE socket

// UDP Receiver
FUNCTION receive_datagram():
 CREATE socket (SOCK_DGRAM)
 BIND socket TO local_port
 
 // Receive any incoming datagram
 packet = RECEIVE FROM socket
 
 // Process packet (no ACK sent automatically)
 PROCESS packet.data
 CLOSE socket
examples.explanation: UDP requires no connection setup. Each datagram is independent with minimal overhead. No state is maintained between packets. The receiver must handle any reliability requirements at application layer if needed.

examples.title: TCP Data Transfer with Sequence Numbers
examples.description: Demonstrates reliable ordered data transfer using sequence numbers and acknowledgments
examples.code:
// TCP Sender
FUNCTION send_data_with_reliability(data, connection):
 segment_size = 1000 // bytes
 current_seq = connection.next_seq
 
 FOR each chunk OF data IN segment_size:
 SEND TCP_PACKET(
 seq=current_seq,
 data=chunk,
 flags=ACK_EXPECTED
 )
 current_seq = current_seq + chunk.length
 
 // Wait for ACK with timeout
 ack_received = WAIT_FOR_ACK(timeout=100ms)
 IF NOT ack_received THEN
 // Retransmit last segment
 RETRANSMIT last_segment
 END IF
 END FOR

// TCP Receiver
FUNCTION receive_data(connection):
 buffer = EMPTY
 expected_seq = connection.expected_seq
 
 WHILE TRUE:
 packet = RECEIVE FROM connection
 
 IF packet.seq == expected_seq THEN
 APPEND packet.data TO buffer
 expected_seq = expected_seq + packet.data.length
 
 // Send cumulative ACK
 SEND TCP_PACKET(flags=ACK, ack=expected_seq)
 ELSE IF packet.seq > expected_seq THEN
 // Out of order - send ACK for last received
 SEND TCP_PACKET(flags=ACK, ack=expected_seq)
 END IF
 END WHILE
examples.explanation: Sequence numbers track data ordering. ACKs confirm receipt. If ACK not received within timeout, sender retransmits. Receiver buffers out-of-order segments and delivers in order.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is data size with retransmissions

examples.title: TCP Connection Termination Pseudocode
examples.description: Demonstrates graceful connection termination using four-way handshake
examples.explanation: Four-way handshake ensures all data is transmitted before closing. FIN indicates no more data to send. TIME_WAIT state ensures delayed packets are processed before completely closing.
